_comment: Ground truth for the NIST CFReDS Data Leakage Case (insider exfil + anti-forensics).
Tier: GREEN (score against) — NIST ships a step-by-step answer key. Maps onto the
Dungan-style insider-exfil starter case. The insider used multiple exfil channels
(USB, cloud, webmail, print) and ran anti-forensic wiping to cover tracks.
We enumerate the channels/behaviors a correct run must surface.

findings.description: USB mass-storage device connected and used to stage confidential data
Cloud storage (e.g. Google Drive) used as an exfiltration channel
findings.artifact_hint: Browser history / cache for cloud-storage uploads

findings.description: Personal webmail used to send confidential files off the host
Anti-forensic wiping/cleaning tool executed to destroy evidence
Confidential documents archived/compressed for staging before exfil
findings.artifact_hint: Archive creation in MFT timeline near exfil events

findings.description: Printing used as an alternate exfiltration channel
findings.artifact_hint: Print spooler artifacts / EMF spool files

findings.description: Deletion of staged files immediately after copy (anti-forensics)
findings.artifact_hint: USN journal delete records following copy events
